title: Expose ontology-aware runtime input/status CLI for external supervisors
description:
## Context

`imm_supervisor` currently decides Codex/OMX state by scraping tmux pane text and injects prompts with local tmux logic. This creates false busy/idle decisions (for example, stale `Ran` lines below a now-idle `›` prompt) and duplicates ccbot runtime-input behavior.

The desired direction is to route both runtime input and runtime state through ccbot core, using ccbot ontology/control-plane semantics instead of ad-hoc supervisor scraping.

Important boundary:

- `ccbot send` is Telegram delivery surface (`ccbot send --help` now exposes that CLI) and must remain text/file delivery to Telegram.
- Runtime/TUI input needs a separate ontology-aware command/API, not `ccbot send`.

## Proposed work

Add a ccbot core runtime-control CLI/API for external supervisors, e.g. `ccbot inject`, `ccbot input`, or `ccbot runtime-send`, plus a state/status query if one does not already exist.

The command/API should:

- resolve the target through existing ccbot state/ontology where possible (`CCBOT_DIR`, surface/window binding, runtime kind, tmux window id);
- use existing live runtime input-plane checks and fail closed when no writable runtime plane is attached;
- reuse existing `RuntimeInputDriver` / `TmuxManager` paths, including Codex multiline `paste-buffer -p -d` + bare `Enter` semantics;
- provide machine-readable output suitable for supervisors (`--json`), including status such as `input_ready`, `busy`, `blocked_prompt`, `read_only`, `no_live_input_plane`, and target/window metadata;
- keep `ccbot send` documented and tested as Telegram delivery, not runtime injection.

## imm_supervisor consumer need

After this lands, `/home/tools/imm/scripts/imm_tmux_supervisor.sh` should stop scraping pane tails for state and stop embedding tmux prompt delivery. It should ask ccbot for runtime state and call the new ccbot runtime-input command when ccbot says the target is writable/input-ready.

## Acceptance criteria

- New ccbot runtime-input/status command or documented API exists and has `--help`.
- `ccbot send --help` remains Telegram delivery help and tests prove it is not runtime injection.
- Runtime input path uses ccbot ontology/live-input-plane guardrails and fails closed on read-only/no live plane.
- Codex multiline prompts use existing paste-buffer + bare Enter behavior through ccbot, not duplicated supervisor code.
- Tests cover CLI contract and at least one no-live-plane/read-only failure.
- Docs/ontology mention the boundary between delivery surface (`ccbot send`) and runtime input plane.
notes: 2026-05-23 UltraQA follow-up from ccbot-64d: binding-preflight now fails closed for helper/inactive/missing runtime metadata, but the long-term supervisor/API work should still unify preflight/status/runtime-input around one authoritative target resolver and add immediate expected-field checks on the write path (or a preflight fingerprint revalidated by runtime-input) to remove TOCTOU between diagnostic preflight and injection.
close_reason: Implemented and verified ontology-aware runtime input/status CLI boundary: runtime-input/inject plus runtime-status/binding-preflight help, machine-readable status JSON, fail-closed no-live-plane behavior, docs/ontology, and targeted tests.

title: Generated image success text is not delivered back to Telegram chat
description:
## Correction
This issue is not about delivering the generated image as a Telegram attachment. The expected outcome was a text success/result message in the Telegram chat after the image generation completed.

## Problem
A coding agent generated an improved sticker/image artifact with `gpt-image-2`, and the runtime showed a successful generation result plus a local file path, but the originating Telegram chat did not receive the final text message reporting success/result/path.

Observed example from @ImmArenaBot flow:
- Tool output reported: `Generated Image`
- Saved file: `/home/tools/imm/.codex/generated_images/019de621-9462-7bd0-b2b6-b0b5fe814107/ig_07505510d95b59320169f5ace6313881919be01593ef043a65.png`
- The user expected Telegram text delivery of the success/result message, not an automatic image attachment.
- No such Telegram text result arrived.

## Expected behavior
When the runtime produces a final user-facing success/result message after image generation, compact Telegram delivery should deliver that final text to the bound/originating chat/thread. The text may include the generated file path or a concise result note.

## Actual behavior
The success/result is visible in the runtime transcript, but the Telegram surface does not receive the final text message.

## Boundaries
- Do not require automatic image attachment delivery for this bug.
- Do not repurpose `ccbot send`; it remains outbound Telegram delivery.
- Do not change runtime/TUI input semantics (`runtime-input`/`inject`).
- Preserve topic/thread binding, compact final-message semantics, and CCBOT_DIR resolution guardrails.
- Do not print or expose Telegram tokens/secrets in diagnostics.

## Initial hypothesis
This is likely a compact delivery/final-message detection or routing gap for generated-image success output in the IMM-controlled runtime, not a missing media attachment feature.
acceptance_criteria: After a generated-image operation completes in an IMM/ccbot runtime, the originating Telegram thread receives the final text success/result message; no automatic image attachment is required for this bug; tests/docs cover the compact delivery contract.
notes:
Work started 2026-05-02 on p2 /home/tools/ccbot feat/runtime-input-cli. Requirements source of truth is the Bead text; no deploy/restart planned.
Implemented and pushed in 08a7136. Verification: 114 passed in tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py; 23 passed in tests/ccbot/test_docs_contracts.py; targeted ruff clean; full pytest 735 passed; desloppify scan/status/next strict 79.0, queue 0, no open findings for telegram_delivery_policy.py or touched tests. Live deploy not run by request.
close_reason: Acceptance verified and implementation pushed in 08a7136; generated-image success text now becomes compact terminal Telegram text without automatic media attachment.

title: Harden ccbot Telegram polling/send retries against transient Bot API timeouts

Verification 2026-05-24: targeted polling/docs tests 5 passed; targeted ruff passed; deploy smoke after ccbot.service restart shows service active, startup restore succeeded, Telegram polling health watchdog started, and no PoolTimeout/TimedOut/error/Traceback matches in post-restart journal.

title: Do not let stale restore runtime override live Codex fd proof
description:
Forensics 2026-05-24: comfy-agent final answer existed in current Codex rollout /data/iqdoctor/.codex/sessions/2026/05/24/rollout-2026-05-24T07-06-24-019e58ce-6f9e-7ea2-8c4d-5273ed295670.jsonl at 09:22:54 UTC, and was visible in tmux @1, but no assistant_final audit row was emitted to Telegram topic t:-1003685295814:555 after the Round 7 question edits.

Root cause evidence: /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot/.env still contains CCBOT_RESTORE_RUNTIME_ID=019e4e71-1499-7d11-991b-2de6af8aa0ce for comfy-agent/t:555. monitor_state tracks old 019e4e71 and does not track current 019e58ce. state.json window_states[@1] is repeatedly overwritten back to old 019e4e71 even though journal logs show live process fd proof adopting @1 from old 019e4e71 to current 019e58ce.

Implementation direction: CCBOT_RESTORE_* should be startup-only/recovery intent, not long-lived runtime authority. When live tmux fd proof or current open rollout evidence disagrees with restore intent for the same window, live proof must win and stale restore must be ignored/expired.
acceptance_criteria:
- Regression covers window @1 with stale CCBOT_RESTORE_RUNTIME_ID and live fd proof for a different current Codex session; resolve_thread_for_window keeps the live session.
- session_monitor tracks the live current rollout session and does not pin to the stale restore id.
- Restart/deploy smoke: comfy-agent t:555/@1 final_answer from current rollout is delivered as assistant_final to Telegram.
- Safe migration/backfill plan documented for clearing stale CCBOT_RESTORE_* in existing /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot/.env without leaking tokens.
close_reason: Fixed in 814aa63: live fd-proven Codex identity now outranks stale restore intent; regression/session-monitor tests pass; deployed ccbot.service and migrated Comfy restore runtime id to current session with backup /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot/.env.backup-20260524T095550Z-ccbot-6g9.

title: Always fence compact command status previews
description:
Forensics on 2026-05-23 comfy audit found some `⌘ Command` status previews delivered without a shell code fence while adjacent command previews were fenced.

Evidence:
- audit line 25295, t:555/@1, message_id=8560: preview `⌘ Command python - <<'PY' preview 1/15 lines`
- audit lines 25306, 25148, 25234: preview `⌘ Command set -euo pipefail preview ...`

`⌘ Command output` rows were formatted, but command previews can degrade to bare text. This is a formatting/ontology drift: a Command bubble should render command payload as a `sh` code block even when the rollout event contains a partial preview/truncated command.
acceptance_criteria: Command_execution/status rendering always wraps command payloads in a shell fence unless the payload is already safely fenced; add regression for bare single-line and truncated preview inputs such as `set -euo pipefail\npreview 1/11 lines` and `python - <<'PY'\npreview 1/15 lines`; audit preview after fix should start with `⌘ Command ```sh` for command statuses.
close_reason: Implemented bare ⌘ Command preview shell-fence normalization without touching ⌘ Command output; regressions pass; full pytest 1020 passed.

title: Stabilize comfy-agent-ops binding names and event routing
description:
Source request analysis recovered from /data/iqdoctor/.codex/history.jsonl on 2026-05-23T07:20Z.

Operator observed a new comfy-agent ops thread/binding flow:
- A new thread intended as comfy-agent-ops was bound to a new OMX thread in /home/tools/mediagen-comfy.
- The Telegram topic/window name became mediagen-comfy from the repository directory name; operator expected the explicitly chosen thread/surface name comfy-agent-ops to win.
- While the topic appeared idle, unrelated/foreign runtime event updates were delivered into the mediagen-comfy topic.

This epic is the cross-repo implementation plan for ccbot. It complements mediagen-comfy documentation work and existing ccbot issues:
- ccbot-n3t: hard-ignore Hermes-owned topics before accept/typing (currently deferred with PRD/spec linked).
- ccbot-64d: canonicalize ComfyCodexBot binding to /home/tools/mediagen-comfy and detect stale server/comfy drift.
- ccbot-o0v / ccbot-gt2 / ccbot-cqt: strict Telegram video delivery evidence.

Plan:
1. Read-only reproduce/inspect: capture binding state, tmux windows/panes, Telegram topic mapping, monitor/audit routing rules, and recent delivery logs without printing tokens.
2. Name resolver fix: preserve user-requested thread/surface name for topic/window display when binding, using cwd only as fallback/workspace metadata.
3. Routing guard fix: outbound progress/final/event-stream delivery must be scoped to the exact bound runtime/session/topic and must fail closed when the runtime is idle, stale, or belongs to another surface.
4. Hermes leakage fix: re-activate or implement ccbot-n3t so Hermes-owned topics 5733/5800 cannot produce ccbot accepted/typing/runtime_input artifacts.
5. Restart/smoke: after tests, restart only the target ccbot service/runtime, verify bindings survived, verify no foreign stream in idle topic, and record message/audit evidence without secrets.
6. Update docs/contracts so future agents know the difference between display name, workspace cwd, Telegram topic id, tmux window id, and runtime session id.
acceptance_criteria:
- Binding model stores/display-preserves the operator requested surface/thread name, with cwd recorded separately.
- Tests cover name fallback order: explicit thread/surface name > existing Telegram topic title > tmux window title > cwd basename.
- Outbound event delivery requires exact runtime/session/topic match and tests prove foreign/stale/idle streams are not delivered.
- Hermes-owned topics are ignored before accept/typing/runtime_input artifacts.
- Restart/smoke evidence shows target ccbot service/runtime healthy, bindings preserved, no token leakage, and no cross-topic event stream after restart.
notes:
UltraQA forensics/spec linked 2026-05-23: PRD .omx/plans/prd-comfy-agent-ops-bind-stream-aliasing.md, test spec .omx/plans/test-spec-comfy-agent-ops-bind-stream-aliasing.md, scenario matrix .omx/ultraqa/comfy-agent-ops-bind-stream-aliasing-matrix.md. Live evidence: t:555->@1 and t:8227->@6 both resolved to Codex thread 019e4e71-1499-7d11-991b-2de6af8aa0ce; @6 was a fresh idle omx --madmax window; delivery audit showed 58 content hashes delivered to both threads since 07:08Z.

Startup restore preserved t:555 -> @1 (comfy-agent). Current state also shows t:8227 -> @6 with window/display mediagen-comfy, matching the operator's name-vs-cwd complaint. No ComfyUI/Hermes service restart was performed.
Implementation evidence 2026-05-23: ccbot-7xy.1 and ccbot-7xy.2 fixed in current branch with chat-qualified surface titles, no cwd-title overwrite on unknown title, duplicate runtime identity fail-closed, and duplicate replay fan-out suppression. Logs prove @8 was created at /home/tools/ccbot at 10:20:09Z and then tracked stale Codex thread 019e3169..., causing foreign ccbot-development replay in topic t:8339. title: Preserve comfy-agent-ops surface name instead of cwd basename
description:
Operator expected a newly created/bound thread named comfy-agent-ops to keep that identity. Instead, the Telegram topic/tmux window surfaced as mediagen-comfy, apparently derived from cwd basename /home/tools/mediagen-comfy.

Problem:
- cwd is workspace metadata, not the human-facing surface/thread identity.
- Overwriting the chosen name makes multi-agent ops ambiguous and encourages cross-topic mistakes.
- The binding record should preserve both: display surface name and workspace cwd.

Implementation direction:
- Audit bind/new-thread/window creation path and resolver order.
- Use explicit user/thread/surface name as primary display identity.
- Use cwd basename only as fallback when no explicit surface/title exists.
- Keep /home/tools/mediagen-comfy as canonical workspace for ComfyCodexBot, but do not rename the operator's ops surface to that basename.
acceptance_criteria:
- Creating/binding a surface named comfy-agent-ops does not rename it to mediagen-comfy solely because cwd is /home/tools/mediagen-comfy.
- Binding state exposes both display_name/surface_name and workspace_cwd or equivalent fields.
- Tests cover explicit name, Telegram topic title, tmux title, and cwd fallback cases.
- Existing canonical workspace drift checks from ccbot-64d remain valid.
notes:
UltraQA evidence 2026-05-23: tmux_manager.create_window defaults fresh window_name to Path(work_dir).name; bot _create_and_bind_window syncs the fresh Telegram topic title to the created window name. For thread 8227 this produced tmux/topic name mediagen-comfy instead of the intended comfy-agent-ops.
2026-05-23 evidence after restart: /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot/state.json has surface_bindings 3045664 t:8227 -> @6 and window_states @6 cwd=/home/tools/mediagen-comfy window_name=mediagen-comfy; tmux list-windows shows comfy:4:mediagen-comfy. This confirms cwd-derived display/name drift for the new ops surface.
Implemented 2026-05-23 in current branch: topic-created/topic-edited titles now persist as chat-qualified surface title metadata; fresh bind uses stored Telegram topic title as tmux window_name; unknown title no longer syncs cwd basename back to Telegram. Evidence: .omx/ultraqa/comfy-agent-bind-stream-aliasing-fix-20260523T074500Z.md; tests: touched slice 243 passed, full suite 942 passed.
Final deploy smoke 2026-05-23T07:58Z: ccbot.service active/running after restart; future fresh topics with captured title will use chat-qualified surface_titles and unknown-title binds will not push cwd basename to Telegram. No additional naming regressions observed in code/test gates.

title: Prevent foreign runtime event stream delivery into idle Telegram topic
description:
Operator observed foreign/unrelated event updates delivered into the mediagen-comfy Telegram topic while the intended comfy-agent ops thread appeared idle.

Problem:
- Progress/final/event delivery is a control-plane safety surface; it must not spray events from another runtime/session/topic into the current topic.
- A quiet topic receiving another stream undermines trust in ccbot binding and makes audit/evidence unreliable.
- This is related to, but broader than, ccbot-n3t: n3t covers inbound ccbot reactions in Hermes-owned topics; this issue covers outbound monitor/delivery routing into the wrong topic.

Implementation direction:
- Audit monitor/replay/event delivery source IDs: Telegram chat/thread, surface key, tmux window/pane, runtime session/thread id, Codex session id, and cwd.
- Require exact binding/session match before delivering progress/final/lifecycle events.
- Treat idle/stale/no-live-plane targets as fail-closed for event delivery.
- Add key=value diagnostic logs that explain suppressed foreign/stale events without leaking message text or tokens.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests prove events from a different runtime/session/surface are suppressed and are not delivered to the bound topic.
- Tests cover idle/stale/no-live-plane state as fail-closed for event delivery unless explicitly replayed by an operator-scoped command.
- Suppression logs include safe routing metadata and never include Telegram tokens or sensitive message bodies.
- Live restart/smoke verifies the target topic receives only its own ACK/progress/final stream after service restart.
notes:
UltraQA evidence 2026-05-23: session_monitor maps bindings through resolve_thread_for_window; fresh @6 adopted the already active @1 Codex thread 019e4e71 via same-cwd/recent rollout resolution, then find_users_for_session fanned the same replay stream to both t:555 and t:8227. Regression target: same-cwd fresh bind must stay replay-silent until unique process/session proof exists.
2026-05-23 evidence after restart: monitor_state.json still tracks Codex thread 019e4e71... and journal after restart emitted session_monitor [complete]/handle_new_message [streaming] for that thread. Treat as live repro evidence that restart alone does not prove cross-stream isolation; implementation must add exact routing guards/fail-closed semantics.
Implemented 2026-05-23 in current branch: duplicate Codex runtime-thread adoption now fails closed when another bound tmux window already owns the thread id; duplicate replay fan-out across distinct windows is suppressed to the authoritative owner. Evidence: .omx/ultraqa/comfy-agent-bind-stream-aliasing-fix-20260523T074500Z.md; tests: codex duplicate identity regressions plus full suite 942 passed.
Deploy observation 2026-05-23T07:48Z found a restore-time edge case: the first owner heuristic let stale @6 appear authoritative because restored @1 got a newer registered_at. Follow-up commit adds startup-restore owner proof and duplicate claim cleanup so the configured restore surface t:555/@1 reclaims thread 019e4e71 and clears stale @6.
Final deploy smoke 2026-05-23T07:58Z: after monitor pass, state has @1 session_id=019e4e71... and @6 session_id cleared; no post-fix telegram_delivery_audit rows for t:8227/@6 in the observed tail. Journal shows @6 resolves ambiguous/replay-silent rather than enqueueing content.
close_reason: Fixed by duplicate runtime thread owner guard and replay fan-out suppression tests; pending deploy smoke in this session.

title: Backfill chat-qualified surface title metadata from legacy topic titles
description: Follow-up from the 2026-05-23 comfy-agent bind fix. The fix intentionally avoids using legacy surface_titles[user][t:<thread_id>] for known group topics, because that legacy key can bleed titles across Telegram groups that share a numeric topic id. If operators created/renamed a topic before the deployed fix and bind it only after deploy, the stored legacy title will not seed the fresh tmux name until Telegram sends another create/edit event. Consider a safe migration/backfill that promotes legacy title entries to chat-qualified t:<chat_id>:<thread_id> only when group_chat_ids proves a unique Telegram chat coordinate, and never guesses across multiple groups.

- Migration/backfill only promotes a legacy title when exactly one safe chat_id coordinate is known for that user/thread.
- Same numeric topic id across multiple groups remains isolated and never shares display metadata.
- Tests cover unique-coordinate promotion and ambiguous multi-group no-op.
- Docs note the legacy compatibility behavior.

title: Debounce renderer-exited OMX question errors during Telegram bridge
description:
Forensics 2026-05-24 around comfy-agent `/home/tools/mediagen-comfy`, Telegram thread `t:555`, tmux window `@1`.

Confirmed race after the ccbot-k6f bridge fix:

- Round 5 first attempt `question-2026-05-24T08-46-31-421Z-476031fa`: record became `status=error` at `08:54:34.716Z` with `question_runtime_failed: Question renderer tmux-pane %69 exited before answering`; ccbot audit then edited Telegram message `9342` to `❌ OMX Question error` at `08:54:35.725Z`.
- The same attempt had `bridge_failed` at `08:54:34.785Z`: `Codex is still working; Telegram input requires an idle/input-ready pane...`.
- Round 5 retry `question-2026-05-24T09-02-13-154Z-0f5f0bb2`: Telegram message `9345` was edited to `❌` at `09:02:41.586Z`, then to `✅ answered` at `09:02:51.929Z`; durable record is ultimately `status=answered` with answer `broad_autonomy`.
- Round 6 had the same short flicker: `❌` at `09:04:45.414Z`, then `✅` at `09:04:46.573Z`.
- Round 7 `question-2026-05-24T09-05-13-823Z-578427e4`: `bridge_failed` at `09:06:06.153Z`, then terminal error edit at `09:06:07.041Z`; durable record remains `status=error` with renderer `%72` exited.

Root cause in current code: `_bridge_answer_to_runtime(..., window_id=@1)` calls `_close_renderer_pane(record, ...)` before `session_manager.send_to_window(...)`. If Codex is busy, or if send/ACK takes several seconds, the renderer pane exits while the durable record is still pending; OMX writes terminal `error`, and status polling renders that terminal state into Telegram. If the bridge later succeeds, ccbot overwrites the same bubble with success, producing visible error flicker.

Desired behavior: once a Telegram callback starts bridging an OMX question answer, renderer-exited `question_runtime_failed` should not become a visible terminal Telegram error immediately. Keep the prompt retryable or show a non-terminal warning until bridge success/failure is known and/or a grace interval expires.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests cover an OMX question callback where the renderer pane is closed before runtime ACK and the durable record temporarily flips to `error`; Telegram must not show `❌ OMX Question error` before bridge resolution.
- If runtime bridge succeeds after a slow ACK, the original Telegram question bubble is edited directly to `✅ answered` with no intermediate visible terminal error.
- If runtime bridge fails because Codex is busy, the Telegram callback alert remains visible and the question stays retryable from Telegram; renderer-exited records with return-target/window proof are treated as recoverable for a bounded grace window.
- Round-style multi-select questions keep existing toggle/edit behavior.
close_reason: Implemented renderer-exited OMX question recovery window. Recent question_runtime_failed records that still name a return pane now remain Telegram-retryable instead of being rendered as terminal errors; stale renderer-exited errors still become terminal. Evidence: tests/ccbot/handlers/test_omx_questions.py 31 passed; status_polling+omx_questions+docs contracts 74 passed; ruff all src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed; git diff --check passed.

title: Deliver current-turn Codex user echo when live tracker attaches late
description:
media-jokes forensic: topic t:-1003685295814:8718 / tmux @16 / Codex session 019e5667-0b1d-7e00-a9b7-e46b749d5e2e missed visible compact user echo for tmux-originated prompt: собери только бриф на тему - анекдот по процессу Citematic Shorts.

Evidence:
- rollout line 7/8 has response_item role=user + event_msg.user_message at 2026-05-23T20:30:16.977Z/978Z.
- ccbot adopted @16 from process fd proof at 20:30:19.977Z and only started tracking rollout source at 20:30:21.650Z.
- session_monitor new tracked source path initializes last_byte_offset to EOF and continues, so records already in the file are skipped.
- delivery audit thread 8718 starts with technical_status message_id=8801 at 20:30:33 and sends assistant_final message_id=8802 at 20:32:20; no semantic_kind=user_echo row exists for the prompt.
- CodexRolloutNormalizer itself can emit a dispatchable user_echo for the same records, so parser/policy/queue are not the primary cause.

Impact: Telegram mirror loses the turn-opening human request for tmux/Codex-originated or late-bound live turns while still showing tool/status/final output, breaking compact ordinary user echo invariants and forensic readability.

Fix direction:
- For new live Codex tracked sources, do not blindly start at EOF. Backfill from a bounded current-turn anchor (registered_at/live_since minus skew, latest turn_context, or first recent non-internal user opener).
- Keep EOF behavior for historical/external attach where old-history flood is undesired.
- Deduplicate Telegram-originated input via FastRuntimeInputProof scoped by window/thread/runtime/text hash; never suppress tmux-originated replay user echo by bare text alone.
- Add suppress/audit rows when prior replay records are intentionally skipped on bind/resume.

Acceptance:
1. Fixture matching media-jokes late attach emits exactly one compact user_echo for the prompt before status/final.
2. Existing fast Telegram input receipt/replay dedupe remains no-duplicate.
3. Old historical attach does not flood past user history.
4. Internal payloads (<subagent_notification>, hook_prompt, turn_aborted) stay hidden.
5. Ontology/docs/tests updated for live replay backfill semantics.
close_reason: Implemented bounded live Codex current-turn user echo backfill with stale/live/internal safeguards; targeted/full pytest and ruff green.

title: Suppress expired Telegram callback ACK errors after slow OMX question answers
description:
Forensics 2026-05-24 showed a secondary error after a successful OMX question answer:

- Round 5 retry (`question-2026-05-24T09-02-13-154Z-0f5f0bb2`) successfully wrote the answer and edited Telegram message `9345` to `✅ OMX Question answered` at `09:02:51.929Z`.
- Immediately after that, PTB logged an unhandled exception from `handle_omx_question_callback` line ~2097: `telegram.error.BadRequest: Query is too old and response timeout expired or query id is invalid` while calling `await query.answer("Answered")`.

This means callback handling can exceed Telegram's callback-query answer window when runtime input bridging is slow. The successful bridge/edit should remain successful, but late callback ACK failures must be caught and logged at debug/info level instead of surfacing as application ERROR.
acceptance_criteria:
- OMX question callback `query.answer(...)` calls tolerate Telegram `BadRequest` for expired/invalid callback query IDs after a successful state transition.
- Tests cover a slow successful answer where Telegram message edit succeeds and callback ACK fails without unhandled application error.
close_reason: Implemented safe callback ACK wrapper for OMX question callbacks. Expired/invalid Telegram callback-query ACK errors after a successful slow state transition are logged and suppressed instead of becoming unhandled application errors. Evidence: test_omx_question_callback_ignores_expired_ack_after_success; omx_questions 31 passed; targeted 74 passed; ruff all passed.

title: Clear oversized Telegram media download failures
description:
Production regression after artifact-first audio/video ingress.

Evidence:
- User saw Telegram warning: `⚠ Could not download audio artifact for Codex.`
- str `imm_arena_bot.service` log at 2026-05-03 11:13:53 UTC: `Failed to download Telegram audio artifact: File is too big`.
- No audio artifact appeared under `/data/iqdoctor/.ccbot-imm_arena_bot/media`.

Hypothesis/root cause:
ccbot allowed audio up to 50 MiB, but Telegram Bot API remote `getFile`/download is limited to 20 MB. Files between those limits reach `media.get_file()` and raise `File is too big`, which ccbot collapsed into a generic download warning.

Boundaries:
- `ccbot send` remains outbound Telegram delivery.
- `runtime-input`/`inject` remain runtime/TUI input CLIs.
- No OpenAI or local OSS ASR integration in this fix.

- Telegram BadRequest/File is too big from get_file or download_to_drive returns the same clear warning instead of generic download failure.
- Files within the effective limit preserve artifact-first forwarding behavior.
- Tests cover audio/video preflight and File is too big regressions plus docs/config contract.
- Fix is committed, pushed, deployed to str, and iqdoctor ccbot services are restarted/verified clean.
